# vectors/cbor-canonical/

Test vectors for the **deterministic CBOR codec** specified in
[RFC-0008 §1.1](https://github.com/Ants-Community/ants/blob/main/spec/RFC-0008-wire-formats.md)
(RFC 8949 §4.2.1 strict deterministic encoding).

## Generated by

[`ants-client/foundation/cbor`](https://github.com/Ants-Community/ants-client/tree/main/foundation/cbor),
commit pinned in `meta.json` of each file (TBD: full pin once CBOR is
feature-complete and the vectors are emitted from a CBOR-encoded
`TestVector` per RFC-0008 §8).

## Format (interim)

While the CBOR encoder is still partial, vector files use **JSON**
for the surrounding metadata + hex-encoded byte arrays for the
binary fields. When CBOR's full type set is implemented, these are
re-emitted as canonical-CBOR `TestVector` objects per RFC-0008 §8 and
the JSON files retained as a developer-friendly mirror.

Each file in this directory corresponds to one primitive (or one
group of negative-rejection vectors) and contains a JSON object:

```json
{
  "primitive": "cbor.<operation>",
  "spec":      "RFC-0008 §1.1, RFC 8949 §<n> major type <m>",
  "vectors": [
    {
      "name":       "human-readable name",
      "input":      "value or hex",
      "input_repr": "what the input value represents",
      "output_hex": "hex-encoded canonical CBOR output",
      "notes":      "what makes this vector interesting"
    },
    ...
  ],
  "negative_vectors": [
    {
      "name":      "human-readable name",
      "input_hex": "hex-encoded malformed input",
      "expected":  "ANTS_ERROR_NON_CANONICAL | ANTS_ERROR_MALFORMED | ANTS_ERROR_OVERFLOW | ...",
      "notes":     "what rule the vector exercises"
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

## Files

| File | Primitive |
|---|---|
| [`uint.json`](./uint.json) | `cbor.encode_uint` / `cbor.decode_uint` — major type 0 |
| [`negint.json`](./negint.json) | `cbor.encode_int` / `cbor.decode_int` — major types 0 and 1 |
| [`bytes.json`](./bytes.json) | `cbor.encode_bytes` / `cbor.decode_bytes` — major type 2 |
| [`text.json`](./text.json) | `cbor.encode_text` / `cbor.decode_text` — major type 3 |
| [`non-canonical-rejected.json`](./non-canonical-rejected.json) | negative vectors: non-shortest-form, indefinite, reserved info |

## Status

`uint.json`, `negint.json`, `bytes.json`, `text.json`,
`non-canonical-rejected.json` are populated as of 2026-05-20 from
the inline test vectors in `foundation/cbor/tests/test_cbor.c`.

Pending (await encoder implementation of the relevant major type):

- `array.json` — major type 4
- `map.json` — major type 5 (including canonical-key-order vectors)
- `tag.json` — major type 6 (RFC-0008 §1.1 reserved tags 0, 32, 42)
- `simple.json` — major type 7 subset (bool, null)
